title: Government Advisory Services | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Government Advisory Services

### Achieve your business goals.
No matter what your immigration program looks like today and no matter where your business goals are taking you next, at some point you’re going to deal with governments.
From challenging unjustified denials through litigation, to responding to government investigations or even advocating to shape government immigration policies to meet your company’s needs, working with governments can be time-consuming and overwhelming.
Fragomen is an influential voice in the realm of immigration regulations and policy worldwide. Many members of our dedicated Government Strategies and Compliance Group served as senior government officials, so they understand the ins and outs of government and help clients to navigate shifting political currents every day.
Our unmatched and bespoke government advisory consultation services can make government processes smoother and more manageable to help you achieve your business goals.

### Respected relationships with governments worldwide.
Fragomen maintains strong working relationships with government authorities around the globe. And since we have been on the other side, we can often anticipate next moves before they happen. When there are emergencies or unexpected results, our ability to work with local authorities may lead to a quicker resolution that is fair and just.
We are also a trusted advisor and often the first port of call for governments seeking input on immigration policy. Fragomen partners are often invited to present testimony on immigration policy and its effect on the business community before various legislatures and organizations, including the United Nations.
Fragomen has government relations resources in each region worldwide.

### Dedicated government strategies team.
Through the experience, relationships and goodwill that the members of our Government Strategies team have developed over decades, they have an unparalleled ability to analyze early warning information about policy developments, adjudication trends and enforcement trends, and to keep your company ahead of them. Many of our top immigration professionals have held important immigration-related government positions in the past, and they know how to approach key decision-makers when our clients need help.
Our Government Strategies group also has dedicated branches that concentrate on enforcement defense , crisis management and litigation in certain jurisdictions .

### Strategic consultation and tailored solutions.
From in-depth government guidance to advocacy support, our experienced government strategies professionals can help you craft targeted and innovative strategies for success.
Our services include:
- Government strategies advice, analysis and policy-level advocacy Representation in government enforcement investigations and audits Advice for complex individual cases Advice on the immigration implications of criminal activity Immigration-related discrimination matters H-1B LCA, I-9 and E-Verify compliance support and federal litigation representation
- Government strategies advice, analysis and policy-level advocacy Representation in government enforcement investigations and audits Advice for complex individual cases Advice on the immigration implications of criminal activity Immigration-related discrimination matters H-1B LCA, I-9 and E-Verify compliance support and federal litigation representation
- Government strategies advice, analysis and policy-level advocacy
- Representation in government enforcement investigations and audits
- Advice for complex individual cases
- Advice on the immigration implications of criminal activity
- Immigration-related discrimination matters
- H-1B LCA, I-9 and E-Verify compliance support and federal litigation representation

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
